title: Problem 4 — Pixar Game Engine: Abstract Classes & Interfaces
description:
Build a mini game engine using abstract classes and interfaces.

The following are PROVIDED as support files (do not redefine them):
  • abstract class GameObject (name, isActive, getters, abstract update())
  • interface Movable     — void move(int dx, int dy); String getPosition();
  • interface Drawable     — String draw();

You must create three concrete classes and one utility method.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
1. class PlayerCharacter extends GameObject
          implements Movable, Drawable
──────────────────────────────────────────────
   Private fields: int x, int y, int speed
   Constructor(String name, int x, int y, int speed)
     — calls super(name), initializes position and speed.
   Methods:
     • move(int dx, int dy)  — adds dx to x, dy to y
     • String getPosition()  → "(<x>, <y>)"     e.g. "(3, 5)"
     • String draw()         → "[Player <n> at (<x>, <y>)]"
     • void update()         — moves by (speed, 0)  (i.e., moves right)
     • int getSpeed()        — getter

──────────────────────────────────────────────
2. class Obstacle extends GameObject
          implements Drawable
──────────────────────────────────────────────
   Private fields: int x, int y
   Constructor(String name, int x, int y)
   Methods:
     • String draw()   → "[Obstacle <n> at (<x>, <y>)]"
     • void update()   — does nothing (obstacles are static)
     • String getPosition()  → "(<x>, <y>)"

──────────────────────────────────────────────
3. class PowerUp extends GameObject
          implements Movable, Drawable
──────────────────────────────────────────────
   Private fields: int x, int y, String effect
   Constructor(String name, int x, int y, String effect)
   Methods:
     • move(int dx, int dy)  — adds dx to x, dy to y
     • String getPosition()  → "(<x>, <y>)"
     • String draw()   → "[PowerUp <n> (<effect>) at (<x>, <y>)]"
     • void update()   — moves by (0, -1)  (floats upward each tick)
     • String getEffect()    — getter

──────────────────────────────────────────────
4. class GameEngine  (static utility)
──────────────────────────────────────────────
   public static String runGameLoop(GameObject[] objects, int ticks)

   For each tick (1 to ticks):
     a) Append a line:  "=== Tick <t> ==="
     b) Call update() on every object.
     c) For each object that is Movable, append:  <n> → <getPosition()>
     d) For each object that is Drawable, append:  <draw()>
   After all ticks, append a summary line:
     "Done: <m> movable, <d> drawable, <total> total"
   (counts are of the original array — a PlayerCharacter counts as
    both 1 movable and 1 drawable)
   Return the full string, lines separated by "\n".

──────────────────────────────────────────────
HINTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• Your file should NOT redeclare GameObject, Movable, or Drawable.
• Make all your classes package-private (no public keyword).
• Use instanceof to check Movable/Drawable inside runGameLoop.
• getPosition() format: "(<x>, <y>)" — note the space after the comma.
